sections.heading: Naval Hospital Okinawa
sections.body: The primary US military hospital on island. Located on Camp Foster (Building 2966). Serves all active duty, dependents, and eligible retirees.

contacts.label: Appointments
contacts.note: Call to schedule. Have your sponsor's SSN and DOD ID ready.

contacts.label: Nurse Advice Line
contacts.note: 24/7. Call before going to the ER if your situation is non-critical.

contacts.label: Emergency Room
contacts.note: On-base ER. For life-threatening emergencies off-base, call 119.

tips: Bring your DOD ID and sponsor's ID to every appointment.
The ER wait times are often long — use the Nurse Advice Line first if possible.
Referrals to off-base Japanese specialists are common and covered by TRICARE.

sections.heading: Japanese Hospitals (Off-Base)
sections.body: Japanese hospitals are high quality but English support is limited. Use for emergencies when on-base care is not accessible.

contacts.label: Okinawa Prefectural Nanbu Medical Center
contacts.note: Largest hospital in southern Okinawa. Limited English.

contacts.label: Chubu Tokushukai Hospital
contacts.note: Central Okinawa. Closest major hospital for Okinawa City / Uruma families.

tips: Download the Google Translate app and enable camera translation before you need it.
Bring your insurance card and passport to any Japanese hospital visit.
TRICARE may cover off-base emergency care — keep all receipts for reimbursement.

sections.heading: On-Base Dental Clinics
sections.body: Military dental clinics are available on most bases. Active duty are seen routinely. Dependents may have limited access — check your TRICARE dental plan.

contacts.label: Camp Foster Dental Clinic
contacts.note: Primary dental clinic for Foster area. Active duty priority.

contacts.label: Kadena Dental Clinic
contacts.note: Serves Kadena AB personnel and dependents.

tips: Dependents often need to use TRICARE Dental Program (TDP) for off-base care.
Schedule a new patient exam as soon as possible — wait times can be several weeks.
Orthodontics for dependents is typically off-base and covered under TDP.

sections.heading: Off-Base Dental (English-Friendly)
sections.body: Several off-base clinics near military housing areas have English-speaking staff and experience with TRICARE.

contacts.label: Mihama Dental Clinic
contacts.note: Chatan area. English-speaking staff. Popular with military families.

tips: Always call ahead to confirm English availability and TRICARE acceptance.
Keep your TRICARE dental ID card with you.
Off-base cleanings are affordable even without insurance — typically ¥3,000–¥5,000.

sections.heading: On-Base Pharmacy
sections.body: The Naval Hospital pharmacy is the primary option for active duty and dependents. Prescriptions through military providers are typically free.

contacts.label: Naval Hospital Pharmacy
contacts.note: Located inside Naval Hospital on Camp Foster.

tips: Refill prescriptions before leaving the US — getting stateside medications can take time.
Some medications available over the counter in the US require a prescription in Japan.
The hospital pharmacy can be busy — use the Express Pharmacy line for refills.

sections.heading: Japanese Pharmacies (Yakkyoku)
sections.body: Japanese pharmacies (薬局) are widely available and well-stocked. Most common brands are available under different names.

contacts.label: Matsumoto Kiyoshi
contacts.note: Largest pharmacy chain in Japan. Locations throughout Okinawa.

contacts.label: Sun Drug
contacts.note: Common chain near military housing areas.

tips: Google Translate camera mode works well for reading Japanese medication labels.
Japanese equivalents exist for most common OTC medications — ask the pharmacist.
Bring a list of any medications you take by their generic name, not brand name.

sections.heading: On-Base Mental Health Services
sections.body: Mental health support is available on base and confidential in most cases. Seeking help is encouraged and supported.

contacts.label: Naval Hospital Behavioral Health
contacts.note: Schedule through the main appointment line.

contacts.label: Military OneSource
contacts.note: 24/7. Free, confidential counseling by phone. Up to 12 sessions.

contacts.label: Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
contacts.note: Press 1 for veterans and service members. Available 24/7.

tips: Military OneSource counseling is completely confidential and does not go in your record.
PCS moves are one of the highest-stress events a military family can experience — it's okay to ask for help.
Fleet & Family Support Center also offers free counseling and adjustment support.

sections.heading: TRICARE Overseas
sections.body: While stationed in Okinawa you are covered under TRICARE Overseas Program (TOP). This works differently from stateside TRICARE.

contacts.label: TRICARE Overseas
contacts.note: Toll-free from US. For Japan, call collect: +1-608-373-1078.

contacts.label: TRICARE Online
contacts.note: Manage appointments, referrals, and claims online.

tips: Register all family members in DEERS before you leave the US.
Keep copies of all off-base medical receipts — submit for reimbursement through TRICARE Overseas.
Pre-authorization is required for most off-base specialist visits.
Your primary care manager (PCM) is assigned at Naval Hospital — update this as soon as you arrive.
